name: X / Twitter
description: Connect X through an X Developer app and OAuth 2.0 User Context for X API/xurl-style tools, with browser-session login still available for UI-only posting flows.

permissions.label: Likes, bookmarks, reposts, follows, lists, Spaces, trends, and DMs when scopes allow
Post, reply, like, repost, follow, list, and DM with explicit approval

fields.label: OAuth 2.0 Client ID (not API Key)
fields.placeholder: OAuth 2.0 Client ID from User authentication settings
fields.help: Do not paste the API Key / Consumer Key here. X OAuth 2.0 has a separate Client ID in Keys and tokens after OAuth 2.0 is enabled.

fields.label: OAuth 2.0 Client Secret
fields.placeholder: OAuth 2.0 Client Secret, if your app has one
fields.help: Use the OAuth 2.0 Client Secret, not the API Secret Key. Public PKCE apps may not require a secret.

fields.label: Callback / Redirect URI
fields.help: This must exactly match one callback URL in the X Developer app User authentication settings.

fields.label: OAuth Scopes
fields.placeholder: tweet.read tweet.write users.read follows.read follows.write like.read like.write bookmark.read bookmark.write list.read list.write mute.read mute.write block.read block.write dm.read dm.write space.read offline.access
fields.help: Request only scopes your X app is allowed to grant. For the full Prometheus surface, configure the X app for Read, Write, and Direct Message permissions.

fields.label: X App Bearer Token
fields.help: Optional app-only token for metadata/future fallback only. User actions still require OAuth 2.0 User Context.
